FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class TitleBar

Namespace
FluentGpu.Controls
Assembly
FluentGpu.Controls.dll

The WinUI 3 TitleBar control (WinAppSDK 1.7, microsoft-ui-xaml controls\dev\TitleBar) over a custom frame (CustomFrame): back + pane-toggle buttons (40w, 16px glyphs), a 16×16 app-identity icon, 12px Caption title/subtitle, a centered content column (the gallery's AutoSuggestBox), and — unlike WinUI, which reserves space for SHELL-drawn caption buttons — three ENGINE-drawn min/max/close CaptionButtons (46w, full bar height, close = shell red) so the whole bar is one GPU-rendered surface.

Non-client plumbing: the control captures its part handles (OnRealized) and, in a layout effect (after layout, before paint — push-on-relayout only, never per frame), reports TitleBarRegions through SetTitleBarRegions: interactive islands (back/pane/content) FIRST as Client, then the three button rects (→ HTMIN/HTMAX/HTCLOSE — the Win11 snap-layouts flyout requires HTMAXBUTTON), then the whole bar as the catch-all Caption drag band (first match wins in WM_NCHITTEST). Window activation/placement changes arrive via the host-bumped WindowChromeEpoch signal: deactivation dims title→tertiary, icon/content→50% opacity, caption glyphs→disabled (the WinUI Deactivated visual state); maximize re-glyphs max↔restore.

public sealed class TitleBar : Component

Fields

BackEnabled

WinUI IsBackEnabled.

public bool BackEnabled

Field Value

bool

CompactHeight

WinUI TitleBar compact mode (no content; reserved — this control always renders tall for now).

public const float CompactHeight = 32

Field Value

float

Content

The centered content column (the gallery's AutoSuggestBox). Invoked per render with the column's AVAILABLE width (DIP) so fixed-width content can clamp itself (WinUI: the content area shrinks first; the caption buttons never move). Return a 0-sized element to collapse when too narrow.

public Func<float, Element>? Content

Field Value

Func<float, Element>

ExpandedHeight

WinUI TitleBar tall mode — the height when Content is set (the gallery look).

public const float ExpandedHeight = 48

Field Value

float

public ColorF IconColor

Field Value

ColorF

IconGlyph

App-identity glyph (the gallery uses the accent grid glyph; WinUI uses an ImageIcon). Empty = none.

The back button root (an IconButton). Owned: OnClick, Role, OnRealized (island capture), Children.

FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
